message:
perf(vdbe): wire mvcc compound page-state helper into shared-txn read path

Both `SharedTxnPageIo::read_page` and `SharedTxnPageIo::read_page_data`
were paying two `page_states` HashMap probes per concurrent-txn page
read — once via `concurrent_page_is_freed` and again via
`concurrent_read_page` — both keyed on the same `PageNumber` under the
same handle lock.

The compound helper `concurrent_page_read_state` (landed in 78350138)
was authored specifically to collapse this pair into one probe, but
its VDBE wiring patch was deferred at the time because `engine.rs`
was reserved by another agent. Land it now, plus the matching
`concurrent_has_page_state` empty-map short-circuit so read-only
transactions skip the compound probe entirely (their `page_states` is
always empty until the first staged write).

Microbench (`bench_read_path_page_state_lookup` in fsqlite-mvcc, n=7
trials × 200,000 reads × 64 staged pages, alternating hits/misses):

  baseline (double-lookup pattern in VDBE): 4.2 ns/probe
  optimised (compound helper):              3.6 ns/probe
  delta: -14.4% per page-state probe

Correctness: identical pre-/post-patch failure count on
`cargo test -p fsqlite-vdbe --lib engine::` (354 passed, 30 failed,
all 30 pre-existing — same set of names). The 14
`shared_txn_page_io_*` tests that directly exercise the changed
methods all pass. Clippy clean. The compound helper preserves the
exact freed-page error semantics: `is_freed` still trips the
DatabaseCorrupt return before `record_read` and before the staged-
data branch.

This addresses the residual `concurrent_page_state` (0.26% MT8
self-time) item flagged in
docs/perf-mvcc-session-20260424.md as "compound-helper caller not
yet wired into every engine site."

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
